name: AREthSetProperty
title: AREthSetProperty
syntax: long AREthSetProperty( char propertyName[], int64 value); // form 1
description: The behavior of the AUTOSAR Eth IL can be configured using properties.

Properties can be set for the current bus context as well as on an object-specific basis for services, Eventgroups, and Events. The default settings for the properties can be obtained from the table below.

parameters.name: Name
parameters.description: Description

parameters.name: ClientId
parameters.description: AREth-ClientId. Included in every message

parameters.name: MaxUDPMessageLength
parameters.description: Sets the maximum allowed size of a SOME/IP message.Maximum size: 65519

parameters.name: SDMulticastIp
parameters.description: If SD is done per IPv4, SD sends multicasts to this IPv4 destination address

parameters.name: SDMulticastPort
parameters.description: SD sends multicasts to this port and, if necessary, opens a local endpoint with this port

parameters.name: SDMulticastIPv6
parameters.description: If SD is done per IPv6, SD sends multicasts to this IPv6 destination address

parameters.name: VlanId
parameters.description: This property may be set in on preStart to select a VLAN which needs to be configured for the current bus context. Furthermore the IL will use the first IP address of this VLAN for ServiceDiscovery and application endpoints that are created by SomeIpOpenLocalApplicationEndpoint.

parameters.name: MajorVersion
parameters.description: Major version of the implemented interface

parameters.name: MinorVersion
parameters.description: Minor version of the implemented interface

parameters.name: SDCyclicOfferDelay
parameters.description: Cycle time in ms

parameters.name: SDConfigurationString
parameters.description: Transmitted as option with OfferService.

parameters.name: SDCyclicRequestDelay
parameters.description: Cycle time in ms

parameters.name: MulticastIp
parameters.description: Events of this EventGroup are sent to this Multicast IPv4 address

parameters.name: MulticastIpv6
parameters.description: Events of this EventGroup are sent to this Multicast IPv6 address

parameters.name: MulticastPort
parameters.description: Events of this EventGroup are sent to this Multicast port

parameters.name: UnicastLimit (deprecated)
parameters.description: 0: only multicast. 1: the first client will be served by unicast and as soon as a second client arrives both will be served by multicast.

parameters.name: MulticastThreshold
parameters.description: 0: only unicast will be used. 1: the first client will be already served by multicast. 2: the first client will be served with unicast and as soon as the second client arrives both will be served by multicast. n: the n-1 first clients will be served with unicast and as soon as the nth client arrives all will be served by multicast.

parameters.name: CycleTimeMs
parameters.description: Strictly speaking, an Event is NEVER sent cyclically, but for test purposes it is sometimes practical for generating traffic.

parameters.name: AliveCounterIncrementationOn
parameters.description: If the value is set to 1, an Alive counter that is responsible for a message is incremented on sending this message and transmitted with this message. This property works only with manufacturer specific databases.

parameters.name: UpdateCRCAndLengthOn
parameters.description: If the value is set to 1, the CRC value, the length and if applicable the so-called Data-ID (see AUTOSAR E2E Library specification) are adapted on sending a message. This property works only with manufacturer specific databases.

parameters.name: SDTTL
parameters.description: TimeToLive in seconds

parameters.name: SDMinInitialDelay
parameters.description: Minimum delay in ms before first message

parameters.name: SDMaxInitialDelay
parameters.description: Maximum delay in ms before first message

parameters.name: SDMaxRepetition
parameters.description: Number or repetitions before cyclic sending

parameters.name: SDBaseRepetitionDelay
parameters.description: Time in ms. Delay between repetitions is then (2^#wdh)*basedelay

parameters.name: SDMinResponseDelay
parameters.description: Minimum delay in ms before sending a response

parameters.name: SDMaxResponseDelay
parameters.description: Maximum delay in ms before sending a response
